chief_complaint: A middle-aged female developed refractory tachycardia during unilateral total knee arthroplasty under general anesthesia.
history: The patient underwent non-thyroidal surgery and was found to have hyperthyroidism via laboratory tests after surgery. She had no known history of thyroid disease prior to the surgery.
exam: Refractory tachycardia during operation, other vital signs and physical examination findings not specified
labs: Laboratory tests confirmed hyperthyroidism, Burch and Wartofsky scoring system suggested thyroid storm
